title_en: The Remarkable Paper Pure Is The Best Way to Take Digital Notes
summary_en: Once you've tried the convenience of digital note-taking, there's no turning back.
body_en:
Editor's Note: This article is sponsored by reMarkable.
While it was once common to have endless pages of notes for meetings, classes, or presentations, the world has changed drastically with digital note software like Microsoft Word and Google Docs. Easy access to these with phones, computers, and tablets makes it a breeze to jump in and begin writing down whatever comes to mind in seconds, removing any need to carry a thick notebook and pen. Digital notes are extremely practical, yet you lose the ability to physically write down each word and organize it however you want on a page. And that's not to mention the constant distractions these devices offer, whether it be Teams, social media notifications, or YouTube.
Instead of choosing between digital and physical notes, what if you could choose the best of both? That's where the reMarkable Paper Pure comes in, a brand-new device that brings the physical feeling of writing down notes with the convenience of digital notes organized across all your devices. With one device, you can write physical notes, access them in the cloud across any device, and cut out distractions where it matters.
reMarkable is no stranger to the paper tablet game, with the Paper Pro as its flagship product for years. While excellent, this device sits at $629, which can be steep if you aren't looking to break the bank. The new Paper Pure cuts down on a few luxury features while still delivering the same great experience, and it's priced hundreds of dollars cheaper at $399.
Paper Pure uses a 10.3-inch monochrome E Ink Carta 1300 display, shifting from the Paper Pro's color display to focus on the essentials. It's perfect for notes, outlines, scripts, planning, and whatever else you're keen on writing down. The surface is made of matte glass with a texture that feels like real paper, so writing feels like it would on any real notebook.
Since reMarkable Paper Pure only functions as a digital notebook, you're free of distractions and can focus on what's in front of you (take that, Microsoft Teams!). You can easily bring in documents to write on from Google Drive and Microsoft 365, and you can just as easily send them to Outlook or applications like Slack. The Paper Pure is rated for up to 3 weeks of battery life as well, so there's no need to constantly bring around a charger like you would for a laptop.
The reMarkable Paper Pure starts at $399, including the Paper Pure and Marker. For $449, you can upgrade to the Paper Pure bundle, which throws in the Marker Plus, Sleeve Folio, and allows you to select between Mist Green, Desert Pink, and Ocean Blue colorways. If you've been on the hunt for a new and distraction-free way to take notes, the Paper Pure is well worth a look.
Noah Hunter is a freelance writer and reviewer with a passion for games and technology. He co-founded Final Weapon, an outlet focused on nonsense-free Japanese gaming (in 2019) and has contributed to various publishers writing about the medium.
